observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant to classify in the books of accounts all
acquired PPE assets in their proper or appropriate accounts
pursuant to the RCA for LGUs. We further recommended and the
Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to
correct the noted misclassifications. The suggested correcting
entry is as follows:

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Require the concerned office/s to speed up the crafting of
programs/projects/activities for the cash award and the share
from charity lotto amounting to ₱1,244,633.00 and ₱30,834.42,
respectively, and implement the same as soon as possible; and
b. Consider the procedures for the proper disposition of dormant
accounts which remained non-moving for more than 10 years
pursuant to Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Conduct a thorough evaluation of the accounts that includes
the issuance of notices to the concerned
bidders/suppliers/contractors and make the appropriate action
based on the results of the evaluation;
b. Request with the Department of Agriculture the release of the
outstanding funds of the four projects; and
c. Strictly abide with the set guidelines on the release of
bid/performance/warranty securities.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to speed up the
crafting of the program/activity for the fund received from the
Department of Agriculture amounting to ₱200,000.00 and
implement the same as soon as possible; and
b. Follow up with the concerned agencies the results of the
confirmations. If the accounts exist in the books of accounts
of the source agencies, revert the said outstanding/unutilized
funds to the respective source agencies pursuant to COA
Circular No. 94-013, otherwise, apply for write off of the
accounts by strictly following the guidelines set in COA
Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that Management conduct the disposal of the
unserviceable assets by strictly following the procedures set in
DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 2024-001 dated January 30, 2024
which prescribes the Revised Manual on the disposal of
government properties.

We recommended and Management agreed to seek the approval of the Sanggunian Bayan of Tublay and the MLGU for the acceptance of the recipient barangays. We further recommended and Management agreed to prepare the necessary transfer documents to support the de-recognition of the transferred MLGU.
observations.managementAction: Summary list of PPAs including road networks funded and/or implemented by the MLGU submitted by MACCO and submitted to the SBO for authorization of transfer/ donation to BLGU (Letter dated 9/27/2024). The SB issued resolution# 128-A Series 2024.
observations.reason: The Barangay Chairmen did not seek yet the authority from their respective Sanggunian to accept the completed road networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory System in recording supplies in the books of accounts the payment of supplies purchase; and
b. Submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories as required under Section 124 of the MNGAS Volume I to facilitate the reconciliation of the inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: All subsequent purchases of supplies are recorded as inventory upon purchased; and Physical Inventory Report was submitted & received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record in the books of accounts the 15 per cent share of the OBO/MEO as Trust Liability under the TF books and the five (5) per cent share of the DPWH as Due to NGAs; and
b. Consider the proposed correcting journal entry: (JEVs#GF Main-JEV#2024-05-001099 & 001249; TF JEVs#2024-05-000096, 000110 and 000116.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to prepare a program of work detailing the manner of utilization of the 15 per cent share for approval by the Municipal Mayor copy furnished the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO) to facilitate the utilization of the fund which has been idle for more than 5 years.
observations.managementAction: Correcting journal entries were made. The 15% share of the OBO/MEO was recorded as Trust Liability under the TF books. The OBO/MEO prepared the Program of work detailing the manner of utilization of the 15 per cent share and was approved by the Mayor. The Identified PPAs were included in the CY 2025 Supplemental AIP since the Annual AIP 2025 was already submitted for review.

For future money placements, management will determine whether such investment is still viable and profitable before investing. For future investments, management will ensure that processes are followed. Management terminated the money placement per SB Res. #96, series 2024 and ADA dated 9/20/24 amounting to 6,284,285.83 (JEV#2024-09-002298).

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to establish a disposal committee to convene and initiate the inspection of the reported unserviceable properties and conduct the disposal procedures in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The mid-year inventory will be organized per Part II of this Restated in Memo # 05 dated July, 2024 after the May 12 elections to facilitate turn-over to newly elected officials. Inventory-taking was conducted by the Committee (Started Oct. 17 - Dec.19, 2024). The unserviceable inventories will be initially appraised by the disposal committee for submission to COA to facilitate disposal after the Midyear Inventory for turn-over to new elective officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit a written explanation on the rationale of executing a deed of sale on May 17, 2023 relative to the procurement of the brand-new rescue vehicle. We likewise recommended that management submit a written justification or explanation on the following, to wit: a) unusual time interval (145 days) of the issuance by the supplier of the revised delivery receipt and the absence or non-usage of the supplier’s letterhead/company header in the revised delivery receipt; and b) delayed payment of the contract which took almost five months after the inspection and acceptance of the vehicle on December 22, 2022. Otherwise, compute the appropriate liquidated damages on the delayed delivery of the rescue vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Submitted written explanation on the rationale of executing deed of sale on May 17, 2023 relative to the procurement of the brand-new vehicle. Submitted a written justification on a) the 145 days Interval of the issuance by the supplier of the revised delivery receipt and the inadvertently omitted/not printed supplier’s letterhead in the revised delivery receipt; and b) delayed payment of the contract. Computed the appropriate liquidated damages on the delayed delivery of the rescue vehicle and forwarded to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to compel the supplier to register the rescue vehicle in the name of the local government of Tublay, otherwise, blacklist the said supplier for his failure to perform his obligation pursuant to Item 4.2 of Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Clarify with the donor the condition set in the Deed of Donation and inform him of the marking requirement under COA Circular No. 75-6 and the prohibition imposed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101. If the donor consents to the removal of the prohibited markings, request that the Deed of Donation be revised to such effect. Else, decline the donation in view of the requirements and prohibitions provided by the COA and DILG Circular/Memorandum Circular; and
b. Fasttrack processing of registration of the vehicle should the donor consent to the alterations required to comply with the prescribed markings of government property and take up accordingly in the accounting records.
We further recommended that management place proper markings on all motor vehicles of the Municipality to conform with the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6.
observations.managementAction: All LGU vehicles owned by MLGU Tublay are properly marked in conformity to COA rules. Once the consent is obtained, the registration process will proceed. Consent has to be obtained before alteration of the markings on the donated vehicle.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that the timeline in the preparation and authorization or approval of the Municipal’s Annual Performance Budget as provided in the Budget Operation Manual for Local Government Units (2023 Edition) is strictly followed to give enough time for the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the Municipality; and

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their RPT and SET records in order to come up with the correct balances of the RPT and SET receivable accounts as well as the other related income accounts; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Municipal Accounting Office at the beginning of the year of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. The list will be the basis of the latter in recording in the books of accounts the RPT and SET receivables for the year as well as in the preparation of subsidiary ledgers for each tax payer.
observations.managementAction:
The MTO is still reconciling with the MASSO to come up with the verified list of Real Property Taxpayers and correct balances of the RPT and SET Receivables, which will be forwarded to the MAccO for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management intensify tax collection campaign program to enhance the collection of RPT/SET receivables through the following:
a. Coordinate with the Provincial Government of Benguet for the implementation of the collection remedies by administrative or judicial action pursuant to Section 256 of RA No. 7160; and
b. Submit to the Sanggunian a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year indicating the reasons for non-collection coupled with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection (Section 269 of RA7160).
We further recommended that management monitor the general revision of the assessment of properties every three years as required under Section 219 of RA 7160, to generate additional income from increased tax assessment.
observations.managementAction:
a. The MTO coordinated with PGB regarding the immediate implementation of the collection remedies by administrative or judicial action.
b. The certified list of all real property tax delinquencies was submitted by the MTO to the SB on March 25, 2024. However, it is not stated in the letter the reasons for non-collection and no request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies was submitted to the SB.
The MAssO would be in constant communication with the provincial office for a smooth and speedy preparation of our SMV for the conduct of real property general revision according to Sec 2019 of RA7160. Presently, the agencies in the province are in the process of preparing the schedule of Base Unit Market Values for the proposed 2025 general revision which was initially submitted to the BLGF but was returned for corrections/computations especially on the filing up of forms as required by BLGF.
observations.reason: The certified list submitted does not state the reasons for non-collection and no request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies was submitted to the SB; general revision pending completion of corrections/computations
